en.extract:
The lacrimose mountain tanager (Anisognathus lacrymosus) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Andean highland forest from Venezuela, through Colombia and Ecuador, to Peru. Some of its 11 subspecies are quite distinctive and A. l. yariguierum was only scientifically described in 2010.
The bird is named Lacrimose (i.e. crying) for the yellow patch below the eye that suggests tears.

The '''lacrimose mountain tanager''' ('''''Anisognathus lacrymosus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Thraupidae]]. It is found in [[Andean]] highland forest from [[Venezuela]], through [[Colombia]] and [[Ecuador]], to [[Peru]]. Some of its 11 [[subspecies]] are quite distinctive and ''A. l. yariguierum'' was only scientifically described in 2010.<ref name=Ridgely2009>{{cite book | author=Ridgely, R.S. | author2=G. Tudor | year=2009 | title=Birds of South America, Passerines | pages=606–607 | isbn=978-1-408-11342-4 }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | author=Donegan, T.M. | author2=J.E. Avendaño | year=2010 | title=A new subspecies of mountain tanager in the Anisognathus lacrymosus complex from the Yariguíes Mountains of Colombia | journal=Bull. B.O.C. | volume=130 | issue=1 | pages=13–32 }}</ref>

The bird is named Lacrimose (i.e. crying) for the yellow patch below the eye that suggests tears.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Friedel |first1=Tom |title=Thraupidae (Tanagers & Allies) |url=https://www.birdsofcolombia.org/colombia/Anisognathus.html |website=Birds of Columbia}}</ref>
	
[[File:Anisognathus lacrymosus pallididorsalis (15636006652).jpg|thumb|left|upright|''A. l. pallididorsalis'' is a pale subspecies from the [[Perijá Mountains]]]]
